mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals labor market normalization, which boosts consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with increasing housing starts indicate synchronized demand expansion across durable goods and residential investment, which lifts corporate profit margins and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward returns across all rolling 3-year windows, or the interaction term coefficient fails to exceed 0.05 in magnitude.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when INDPRO growth decelerates, contradicting the positive supply-driven valuation premise.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent signals robust aggregate demand, which directly translates to higher corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when GDP growth consistently exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold for three consecutive quarters, invalidating the demand-earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rapid payroll growth and accelerating consumer price inflation trigger expectations of premature monetary tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in at least 60% of observed instances, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in housing starts reflects improving household balance sheets and construction sector momentum, which stimulates related industrial demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical difference between months with rising versus falling HOUST values, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the nominal federal funds rate exceeds the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, real borrowing costs become restrictive, which suppresses corporate valuation multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The real rate threshold fails to produce negative forward returns in more than 45% of triggered periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when equity risk premium data is included.
